name: Opportunity Cost
description: The value of the next best alternative that must be given up.

name: Inversion
description: Consider the opposite: what would cause failure, then avoid those paths.

name: Regret Minimization
description: Choose the option you would least regret when looking back from old age.

name: Premortem
description: Imagine the project has failed; work backward to identify causes and mitigate them.

name: Second-Order Thinking
description: Consider the consequences of the consequences of your decisions.

name: Skeptical Risk Manager
description: Focuses on downside protection and failure modes.
worldview: Assume things can go wrong; plan for failure modes first.

name: Optimistic Builder
worldview: Focus on what could go right and how to get there.

name: Practical Operator
description: Grounds decisions in constraints, resources, and feasibility.
worldview: What can we actually do with what we have?

name: Roundtable
description: Each perspective speaks in turn; no formal opposition, emphasis on synthesis.

name: Red Team / Blue Team
description: Structured opposition: one side argues for, one against; stress-test the decision.

name: Steelman Debate
description: Each side must argue the strongest version of the other's position before arguing their own.

name: Inner Council
description: Multiple internal voices (personas) deliberate as a council; single decision-maker hears all.

name: Scenario Planning
description: Explore multiple futures; each scenario gets argued and stress-tested.

name: Career Decision
description: Choices about role, path, or major professional transitions.

name: Resource Allocation
description: How to allocate limited time, money, or attention across options.

name: Strategy
description: Direction, positioning, or multi-step plans under uncertainty.

name: Risk Assessment
description: Evaluating downside, failure modes, and mitigation.
